summary: Import-time multi-channel data exfiltration via console monkey-patch
details: Package's declared main (log.js) is an IIFE that, on require()/import, globally overrides console.log/error/warn to exfiltrate their arguments to an attacker-controlled Telegram bot (hardcoded token 989543891:AAF... with chat_ids -1001161709623/-1001433099398) the analysis found that and PATCHes console.warn payloads to an attacker Firebase DB (iiilll.firebaseio.com) the analysis found that. Sibling files (t.js, cdn.js, etc.) reuse the same Telegram token and embed Cloudflare account/KV IDs and Google Apps Script endpoints, confirming this is a harvesting toolkit rather than a logging library. log.js exports nothing (module.exports commented out), so there is no legitimate functionality — only side effects. This matches the import-time exfiltration and hardcoded-secret-in-shipped-code attack patterns with three independent signals (Telegram channel, Firebase channel, credential bundle).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
